name: Cheese-Stuffed Manicotti
recipeIngredient: Ricotta cheese, 2 pounds (or 2 15 oz cans), diced
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients thoroughly for the filling.
2. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add 2 tablespoons oil to prevent pasta from sticking.
3. Add half of the manicotti shells and cook for 6 to 8 minutes, or until not quite al dente. Stir occasionally with a long-handled fork.
4. Using a slotted spoon, remove 1 or 2 manicotti at a time from the boiling water and place them on a clean kitchen towel to drain. Repeat until all shells are cooked and completely dry.
5. Generously butter a baking dish, 13 x 10 inches.
6. Fill each shell with some of the stuffing using a pastry tube and large nozzle or a regular teaspoon, allowing a little stuffing to come out at both ends.
7. Arrange stuffed manicotti side by side in the buttered dish and surround them with any shells lacking sufficient stuffing.
8. Pour half the tomato sauce over the shells and sprinkle with 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese.
9. Bake in a preheated moderate oven for 15 to 20 minutes.
10. Serve with additional Parmesan cheese and remaining tomato sauce.
